Description: The final date at which this set of targeted coverages should be applied (expiration).
The nested JSON of the actual intervention to be distributed by this event coordinator. See [example](../json/campaign-referencetrackingeventcoordinatortrackingconfig-intervention-config.json).

A list of the **NodeProperty** key:value pairs, as defined in the demographics file, that nodes must have to be targeted by the intervention. See [IndividualProperties](parameter-demographics.md#nodeproperties-and-individualproperties) parameters for more information.

You can specify AND and OR combinations of key:value pairs with this parameter. See [example](../json/common-node-property-restrictions.json).

A list of the **IndividualProperty** key:value pairs, as defined in the demographics file, that individuals must have to be targeted by this intervention. See [IndividualProperties](parameter-demographics.md#nodeproperties-and-individualproperties) parameters for more information.

To specify AND and OR combinations of key:value pairs, use **Property_Restrictions_Within_Node**. You cannot use both of these parameters in the same event coordinator. See [example](../json/common-property-restrictions.json).

A list of the **IndividualProperty** key:value pairs, as defined in the demographics file, that individuals must have to be targeted by this intervention. See [IndividualProperties](parameter-demographics.md#nodeproperties-and-individualproperties) parameters for more information.

This parameter allows you to specify AND and OR combinations of key:value pairs. You may specify individual property restrictions using either this parameter or **Property_Restrictions**, but not both. See [example](../json/common-property-restrictions-within-node.json).
The upper end of ages targeted for an intervention, in years.
The lower end of ages targeted for an intervention, in years.

The target demographic group. Possible values are:

* Everyone
* ExplicitAgeRanges
* ExplicitAgeRangesAndGender
* ExplicitGender
* ExplicitDiseaseState

Specifies the gender restriction for the intervention. Possible values are:

* Male
* Female
* All
When set to true (1), the intervention is only distributed to individuals that began the simulation in the node (i.e. those that claim the node as their residence).
Be more selective of individuals by using the [Targeting_Config classes](parameter-campaign-targeting-config.md). See [example](../json/common-targeting-config.json).

The years (times) and matching values of coverages. This parameter uses **InterpolatedValueMap** to create a JSON structure containing one array of **Times** and one for **Values**, which allows for a time-variable probability that can take on any shape over time. When queried at a simulation year corresponding to one of the listed **Times**, it returns the corresponding **Value**. The **Times** and **Values** must be of equal length, and can consist of a single value. **Times** must monotonically increase. See [example](../json/campaign-referencetrackingeventcoordinatortrackingconfig-time-value-map.json).

Defines the attribute to be tracked within the targeted group; the intervention will be distributed to people without the attribute, if coverage is below the target level the time of polling. See [example](../json/campaign-referencetrackingeventcoordinatortrackingconfig-tracking-config.json).
